@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTC9WJ2TT6GTTT6ZAXVX4XFN
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9180'
+project: semantic-search
+title: 'Kanban search real-pipeline e2e: semantic + exact-identifier + filter scoping'
+---
+## What
+Add a real-pipeline end-to-end test for `search tasks` that proves the whole kanban-search feature works through the REAL path: real tasks created through the real kanban dispatch, embedded via the REAL `EmbeddingCache` + `Embedder`, ranked by the REAL `search tasks` op. NOT fixture-only — do NOT raw-insert vectors into the sidecar; let the op embed them.
+
+New test file: `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/search_tasks_e2e.rs` (a `tests/` integration test; the crate already runs integration tests under `tests/`, e.g. `perspective_migration.rs`). Use the crate's `test-support` feature (already enabled for the crate's own tests/dev-deps) and a `TempDir` board.
+
+Test shape (gate the real-model tests behind the same convention `swissarmyhammer-embedding` uses for model-dependent tests — see the embedding crate's `embedding-models` feature / serial gating; the GPU/model availability mirrors the code-context `semantic_search_e2e.rs` reference):
+- Create a real board via the real dispatch (`parse_input` + `execute_operation`, or `InitBoard`/`AddTask` builders) with several tasks. Include one task whose title contains a distinctive identifier (e.g. `"reticulate_splines refactor"`) plus decoy tasks of ordinary prose that are semantically nearer to a paraphrased query but lexically unrelated to the identifier.
+- Run `search tasks` (through `execute_operation` so it goes via the registered op) with:
+  1. A SEMANTIC query (paraphrase) — assert a semantically-related task ranks highly (cosine contributing).
+  2. An EXACT/typo identifier query (e.g. `"reticulate splines"` or `"reticulate_splne"`) — assert the identifier task ranks `matches[0]`, and assert its `signals.bm25 > 0.0` or `signals.trigram > 0.0` while it is NOT the top cosine — proving fusion (not cosine alone) drove the rank.
+- DSL filter scoping: tag/scope a subset and run `search tasks` with a `filter` (e.g. `#bug`); assert ONLY in-scope tasks appear in results (out-of-scope tasks are excluded before ranking).
+- Cache behavior: after the first `search tasks`, assert the sidecar `<root>/search-cache.sqlite3` exists and has rows; a second call must reuse them (no re-embed) — assert via timing-independent means (row count stable / a seeded-cache spy), not wall-clock.
+
+If a full real-model run is unavailable in CI, also include a model-free variant (build the corpus + run the op forcing `embedding: None` path) that asserts the exact-identifier and filter-scoping behavior via bm25/trigram alone, so the feature has deterministic coverage that always runs. The real-embedder variant carries the cosine/semantic assertion and is gated.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] New e2e test exists at `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/search_tasks_e2e.rs`, runs through the real kanban dispatch + real `search tasks` op (no raw-inserted vectors).
+- [ ] The exact/typo identifier query ranks the identifier task `matches[0]` when pure cosine would not, and the test asserts `signals.bm25`/`signals.trigram` non-zero (fusion proven).
+- [ ] A DSL `filter` correctly scopes the corpus so out-of-scope tasks are absent from results.
+- [ ] The sidecar cache is created and populated by the op; a second call reuses it.
+- [ ] A deterministic, always-run model-free variant covers the exact-identifier + filter-scoping behavior via bm25/trigram.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban --test search_tasks_e2e` passes (model-free variant always; real-embedder variant under the gated feature/serial convention).
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing model-free e2e first (it fails on trunk because `search tasks` does not exist / mis-ranks), then rely on the implemented op to make it pass; add the gated real-embedder variant last.
\ No newline at end of file
